title: Introduction to CamelRatiosIndex
text: CamelRatiosIndex package implements multivariate-weighted indexing method proposed Ayimah et al. (2023a, 2023b) bank performance assessment using CAMEL framework. package provides: camel_index(): Computes composite year--year indices CAMEL ratio data plot_camel_index(): Visualizes percentage differences across banks using ggplot2 Built-data: Example datasets Ghanaian commercial banks (2015-2022) composite index intended offer regulators policymakers standardised, objective monitoring bank performance time across institutions. ability benchmark banks common base year enhances early-warning capabilities, enabling supervisory authorities identify emerging weaknesses individual banks well systemic vulnerabilities within industry.

title: Introduction to CamelRatiosIndex
text: CAMEL internationally recognized framework evaluating bank performance, comprising five dimensions:

title: Introduction to CamelRatiosIndex
Introduction to CamelRatiosIndex
code: library(CamelRatiosIndex)  # Load built-in example data data("camel_2015") data("camel_2022")  # Compute the index result <- camel_index(camel_2015, camel_2022) #> ℹ Using 3 factors (Kaiser criterion suggests 2 for base year).  # View the main output result$index_table #> # A tibble: 21 × 3 #>    bank      I_mw      PD #>    <chr>    <dbl>   <dbl> #>  1 Absa    116.    16.1   #>  2 AB      327.   227.    #>  3 ADB     154.    54.1   #>  4 BA       99.6   -0.420 #>  5 CB        3.72 -96.3   #>  6 Ecobank 549.   449.    #>  7 FBN     171.    70.9   #>  8 FB      151.    50.6   #>  9 FAB     204.   104.    #> 10 FNB     144.    43.7   #> # ℹ 11 more rows

title: Introduction to CamelRatiosIndex
text: matrices, supply bank names separately:
code: base_mat <- as.matrix(camel_2015[, -1]) curr_mat <- as.matrix(camel_2022[, -1]) banks <- camel_2015$Bank  result2 <- camel_index(base_mat, curr_mat, bank_names = banks) #> ℹ Using 3 factors (Kaiser criterion suggests 2 for base year).

title: Introduction to CamelRatiosIndex
text: camel_index() function returns rich object multiple components:
code: # Print overview print(result) #>  #> ── CAMEL Index Results ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> Base year factor analysis: 2 eigenvalue(s) > 1 #> Current year factor analysis: 2 eigenvalue(s) > 1 #> Factors extracted: 3 #>  #> ── Index Table ── #>  #> # A tibble: 21 × 3 #>    bank      I_mw      PD #>    <chr>    <dbl>   <dbl> #>  1 Absa    116.    16.1   #>  2 AB      327.   227.    #>  3 ADB     154.    54.1   #>  4 BA       99.6   -0.420 #>  5 CB        3.72 -96.3   #>  6 Ecobank 549.   449.    #>  7 FBN     171.    70.9   #>  8 FB      151.    50.6   #>  9 FAB     204.   104.    #> 10 FNB     144.    43.7   #> # ℹ 11 more rows #> ── Communality Weights (Base Year) ── #> # A tibble: 5 × 2 #>   ratio  weight #>   <chr>   <dbl> #> 1 Ratio1  0.790 #> 2 Ratio2  0.705 #> 3 Ratio3  0.885 #> 4 Ratio4  0.894 #> 5 Ratio5  0.827 #> ── Summary Statistics ── #> Mean I_mw: 160.05 #> Mean PD: 60.05% #> Best performing bank: Ecobank (PD = 448.77%) #> Worst performing bank: CB (PD = -96.28%)  # Detailed summary summary(result) #>  #> ── CAMEL Index Summary ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> ── Eigenvalues (Base Year) ── #>  #> # A tibble: 5 × 3 #>   component eigenvalue variance_pct #>   <chr>          <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1 PC1            2.16         43.3  #> 2 PC2            1.26         25.1  #> 3 PC3            0.966        19.3  #> 4 PC4            0.324         6.48 #> 5 PC5            0.291         5.83 #> ── Eigenvalues (Current Year) ── #> # A tibble: 5 × 3 #>   component eigenvalue variance_pct #>   <chr>          <dbl>        <dbl> #> 1 PC1            2.06         41.1  #> 2 PC2            1.43         28.7  #> 3 PC3            0.788        15.8  #> 4 PC4            0.524        10.5  #> 5 PC5            0.199         3.97 #> ── Factor Loadings (Base Year) ── #> # A tibble: 5 × 4 #>   ratio   Factor1  Factor2 Factor3 #>   <chr>     <dbl>    <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1 Ratio1  0.835    0.0532    0.302 #> 2 Ratio2  0.766    0.00678  -0.343 #> 3 Ratio3 -0.162    0.920     0.114 #> 4 Ratio4 -0.00290  0.0102    0.946 #> 5 Ratio5 -0.469   -0.762     0.160 #> ── Index Distribution ── #> # A tibble: 7 × 3 #>   statistic   I_mw      PD #>   <chr>      <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1 Min         3.72 -96.3   #> 2 Q1         99.6   -0.420 #> 3 Median    144.    43.7   #> 4 Mean      160.    60.1   #> 5 Q3        171.    70.9   #> 6 Max       549.   449.    #> 7 SD        115.   115.

title: Introduction to CamelRatiosIndex
text: I_mw: Composite index (base = 100). Values > 100 indicate improvement; < 100 indicate decline. PD: Percentage difference base year. Positive = improvement. mw_lasp: Laspeyres-type index using base year communality weights. mw_pash: Paasche-type index using current year communality weights. weights_base/current: Communality values robust factor analysis, representing proportion variance explained CAMEL ratio.

title: Introduction to CamelRatiosIndex
text: index computation follows steps: Transform ratios: Invert Aq, , Lm higher values always indicate better performance. Compute correlations: Build correlation matrices base current years. Extract eigenvalues: Determine number factors (eigenvalues > 1). Robust factor analysis: Use [robustfa::FaCov()] OGK covariance estimation. Extract communalities: Use communalities weights CAMEL ratio. Compute indices: Calculate Laspeyres Paasche indices, average . Scale base 100: Final composite index percentage difference.

title: Introduction to CamelRatiosIndex
text: Ayimah, J. C., Mettle, F. O., Nortey, E. N., & Minkah, R. (2023a). Robust Multivariate Weighting Technique Computing Measure Inflation. African Journal Technical Education Management, 3(1), 1-15. Ayimah, J.C. (2023b). Computing Multivariate-Weighted Consumer Price Index: Application Manual R. B P International. DOI: 10.9734/bpi/mono/978-81-19315-32-1

title: Multivariate-Weighted Indexing of CAMEL Ratios for Bank Performance
text: CamelRatiosIndex implements multivariate-weighted indexing method bank performance assessment using CAMEL framework. package computes composite year--year indices enable: Comparison across multiple banks Assessment bank health relative base year Evaluation overall banking industry health Based methodology proposed Ayimah et al. (2023a, 2023b). composite index intended offer regulators policymakers standardised, objective monitoring bank performance time across institutions. ability benchmark banks common base year enhances early-warning capabilities, enabling supervisory authorities identify emerging weaknesses individual banks well systemic vulnerabilities within industry.

title: Multivariate-Weighted Indexing of CAMEL Ratios for Bank Performance
text: can install package : development version Github using:

title: Multivariate-Weighted Indexing of CAMEL Ratios for Bank Performance
code: library(CamelRatiosIndex)  # inspect example datasets head(camel_2015)   # used as base year's data head(camel_2022)   # used as current year's data  # Compute CAMEL index result <- camel_index(camel_2015, camel_2022)  # View results result$index_table #> # A tibble: 21 x 3 #>    bank      I_mw    PD #>    <chr>    <dbl> <dbl> #>  1 Absa     102.5  2.52 #>  2 AB        98.3 -1.72 #>  3 ADB      101.8  1.78 #>  ...  # Visualize plot_camel_index(result, highlight_banks = c("Absa", "Ecobank", "GCB"))

title: Multivariate-Weighted Indexing of CAMEL Ratios for Bank Performance
text: Tidyverse-native: Built dplyr, ggplot2, tibble Flexible input: Accepts data frames matrices bank names Robust statistics: Uses OGK robust covariance estimation via robustfa Rich output: Returns index table, weights, eigenvalues, factor analysis objects Publication-ready plots: ggplot2-based visualization customizable themes Built-data: Example datasets 21 Ghanaian commercial banks

what: CamelRatiosIndex: Multivariate-Weighted Indexing of CAMEL Ratios for Bank Performance — CamelRatiosIndex-package
title: CamelRatiosIndex: Multivariate-Weighted Indexing of CAMEL Ratios for Bank Performance — CamelRatiosIndex-package
text: Computes composite year--year index bank performance assessment using CAMEL framework (Capital Adequacy, Asset Quality, Management Efficiency, Earnings, Liquidity). multivariate weighting scheme employs factor analysis robust covariance estimation derive communality-based weights correlation matrix CAMEL ratios. Provides functions index computation, visualization, comparison across banks time periods.

title: CamelRatiosIndex: Multivariate-Weighted Indexing of CAMEL Ratios for Bank Performance — CamelRatiosIndex-package

title: Compute Multivariate-Weighted CAMEL Index — camel_index
text: Computes composite year--year index bank performance assessment using CAMEL framework. multivariate weighting scheme employs robust factor analysis derive communality-based weights correlation matrix CAMEL ratios.

title: Compute Multivariate-Weighted CAMEL Index — camel_index
code: camel_index(   base_data,   current_data,   bank_names = NULL,   n_factors = 3,   scale_data = TRUE,   cov_control = rrcov::CovControlOgk(),   method = "pca",   scores_method = "regression" )

title: Compute Multivariate-Weighted CAMEL Index — camel_index
text: base_data data frame matrix containing base year CAMEL ratios. data frame, first column must contain bank identifiers (character numeric). matrix, bank identifiers must supplied separately via bank_names. current_data data frame matrix containing current year CAMEL ratios, format order base_data. bank_names character numeric vector bank identifiers. Required base_data current_data matrices. Must length number rows data. Ignored inputs data frames. n_factors Integer specifying number factors extract robust factor analysis. Default 3. scale_data Logical indicating whether standardize data factor analysis. Default TRUE. cov_control control object robust covariance estimation, passed robustfa::FaCov(). Default rrcov::CovControlOgk(). method Character specifying factor analysis method. Default "pca" (principal component analysis). See robustfa::FaCov() options. scores_method Character specifying method computing factor scores. Default "regression". See robustfa::FaCov() options.

title: Compute Multivariate-Weighted CAMEL Index — camel_index
text: list class "camel_index" containing: index_table tibble::tibble() columns bank, I_mw (composite index, base = 100), PD (percentage difference base). mw_lasp Numeric vector multivariate-weighted Laspeyres indices. mw_pash Numeric vector multivariate-weighted Paasche indices. I_mw Numeric vector composite indices (base = 100). PD Numeric vector percentage differences base year. weights_base Numeric vector communality-based weights base year factor analysis. weights_current Numeric vector communality-based weights current year factor analysis. eigenvalues_base Numeric vector eigenvalues base year correlation matrix. eigenvalues_current Numeric vector eigenvalues current year correlation matrix. n_factors_base Integer, number eigenvalues > 1 base year. n_factors_current Integer, number eigenvalues > 1 current year. fa_base fitted robustfa::FaCov() object base year. fa_current fitted robustfa::FaCov() object current year. relativity_data Matrix current--base ratios CAMEL variable bank. base_data processed base year data (matrix, bank names). current_data processed current year data (matrix, bank names). bank_names Character vector bank identifiers. n_factors Integer, number factors used. call matched call.

title: Compute Multivariate-Weighted CAMEL Index — camel_index
text: index computed arithmetic mean two multivariate-weighted Laspeyres-type Paasche-type indices, scaled base 100. percentage difference (PD) base year also reported.

title: Compute Multivariate-Weighted CAMEL Index — camel_index
text: supplying data frames, first column must bank identifier (character numeric), remaining columns must five CAMEL ratios standard order: Capital Adequacy (Ca) Asset Quality (Aq) – inverted internally Management Efficiency () – inverted internally Earnings (Eq) Liquidity (Lm) – inverted internally inversion Aq, , Lm handled automatically higher values ratios indicate worse bank performance.

title: Compute Multivariate-Weighted CAMEL Index — camel_index

what: Plot CAMEL Index Percentage Differences — plot_camel_index
title: Plot CAMEL Index Percentage Differences — plot_camel_index
text: Creates ggplot2 line graph showing percentage difference (PD) base year bank, enabling visual comparison bank performance across CAMEL framework.

title: Plot CAMEL Index Percentage Differences — plot_camel_index
code: plot_camel_index(   x,   highlight_banks = NULL,   add_reference_line = TRUE,   point_size = 3,   line_size = 0.8,   colour_palette = NULL,   title = NULL,   subtitle = NULL,   caption = NULL,   theme_fn = ggplot2::theme_minimal,   ... )  # S3 method for class 'camel_index' autoplot(object, ...)

title: Plot CAMEL Index Percentage Differences — plot_camel_index
text: x object class "camel_index" returned camel_index(). highlight_banks Optional character vector bank names highlight distinct colours. banks shown grey. add_reference_line Logical indicating whether add horizontal reference line PD = 0 (base year level). Default TRUE. point_size Numeric, size points. Default 3. line_size Numeric, size line segments. Default 0.8. colour_palette Character vector colours highlighted banks. Default uses ColorBrewer qualitative palette. title Optional plot title. NULL (default), descriptive title generated. subtitle Optional plot subtitle. caption Optional plot caption. NULL (default), caption describing base year generated. theme_fn ggplot2 theme function. Default ggplot2::theme_minimal(). ... Additional arguments passed ggplot2::geom_line() ggplot2::geom_point(). object object class "camel_index" (autoplot generic).

title: Plot CAMEL Index Percentage Differences — plot_camel_index
text: ggplot object.

title: Plot CAMEL Index Percentage Differences — plot_camel_index
code: # Basic plot result <- camel_index(camel_2015, camel_2022) #> ℹ Using 3 factors (Kaiser criterion suggests 2 for base year). plot_camel_index(result)   # Highlight specific banks plot_camel_index(result, highlight_banks = c("Absa", "Ecobank", "GCB"))   # Custom styling plot_camel_index(   result,   highlight_banks = c("Absa", "Ecobank"),   title = "Bank Performance: 2015 vs 2022",   subtitle = "Percentage difference from base year",   color_palette = c("#E41A1C", "#377EB8"),   theme_fn = ggplot2::theme_bw ) #> Warning: Ignoring unknown parameters: `colour_palette` #> Warning: Ignoring unknown parameters: `colour_palette`

title: CamelRatiosIndex (development version)
text: Initial CRAN release. Added camel_index() computing multivariate-weighted CAMEL indices. Added plot_camel_index() ggplot2-based visualization. Added print.camel_index() summary.camel_index() methods. Added built-datasets camel_2015, camel_2016, camel_2017, camel_2018, camel_2019, camel_2020, camel_2021 camel_2022. Set CI/CD GitHub Actions. Added package website pkgdown.
